XGSetCubeTextureHeader

Manually creates a cube texture resource.

DWORD WINAPI XGSetCubeTextureHeader(
  UINT EdgeLength,
  UINT Levels,
  DWORD Usage,
  D3DFORMAT Format,
  D3DPOOL Pool,
  IDirect3DCubeTexture8 *pCubeTexture,
  UINT Data,
  UINT Pitch
);

Parameters

EdgeLength
[in] Size of the edges of all the top-level faces of the cube texture. The pixel dimensions of subsequent levels of each face will be the truncated value of half of the previous level's pixel dimension (independently). Each dimension clamps at a size of 1 pixel. Thus, if the division by 2 results in 0 (zero), 1 will be taken instead.
Levels
[in] The number of levels in each face of the cube texture. If this is zero, Direct3D will generate all cube texture sub-levels down to 1×1 pixels for each face.
Usage
[in] Ignored.
Format
[in] Member of the D3DFORMAT enumerated type, describing the format of all levels in all faces of the cube texture.
Pool
[in] Ignored.
pCubeTexture
[in, out] Address of the IDirect3DCubeTexture8 interface, representing the cube texture resource to be filled.
Data
[in] Offset to the data held by this resource.
Pitch
[in] Specifies the pitch of the texture.

Return Values

Returns the number of bytes required by the resource, not counting the header.

Remarks

All resources, including cube textures, include a header that contains all of the information needed by the driver to process the resource and a data allocation that contains information used by the GPU. The resource headers are used only by the driver and so should be allocated from normal cached memory. The header contains all of the information needed by the driver to set up the hardware so that it can use the information stored in the data allocation.

Unless the title is manually creating a resource, it is recommended that the title use IDirect3DDevice8::CreateCubeTexture.

After calling XGSetCubeTextureHeader, it is necessary to call IDirect3DResource8::Register in order to register the memory address of the resource.

Cube textures differ from other surfaces in that they are collections of surfaces. In order to call SetRenderTarget with a cube texture, you must select an individual face using IDirect3DCubeTexture8::GetCubeMapSurface and pass the resulting surface to SetRenderTarget.

A texture (mipmap) is a collection of successively downsampled (mipmapped) surfaces. On the other hand, a cube texture (created by CreateCubeTexture) is a collection of six textures (mipmaps), one for each face. All faces must be present in the cube texture. Also, a cube map surface must be the same pixel size in all three dimensions (x, y, and z).

Requirements

  Header: Declared in xgraphics.h.
  Import Library: Use xgraphics.lib.
